channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: The expert on 'super aging' breaks down the science — and grift — in anti-aging
item.description: From peptides and protein to sleep hygiene and vaccines, what actually helps you age well? Physician Eric Topol breaks down the science — and the myths — of longevity and anti-aging.
item.content:encoded:
From peptides and protein to sleep hygiene and vaccines, what actually helps you age well? Physician Eric Topol breaks down the science — and the myths — of longevity and anti-aging.

(Image credit: Capuski)
item.dc:creator: Manoush Zomorodi

item.title: Former Surgeon General Jerome Adams on Trump's new pick for the role
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former Surgeon General Dr. Jerome Adams about President Trump's latest nominee for the role, Dr. Nicole Saphier.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former Surgeon General Dr. Jerome Adams about President Trump's latest nominee for the role, Dr. Nicole Saphier.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Taking a look at looksmaxxing — and what parents should know
item.description: The trend among boys and young men of optimizing their physical appearance includes dangerous practices. Experts offer advice on how to talk to their sons about body image and healthy behaviors.
item.content:encoded:
The trend among boys and young men of optimizing their physical appearance includes dangerous practices. Experts offer advice on how to talk to their sons about body image and healthy behaviors. 

(Image credit: Malte Mueller)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: It's Day 1 of Medicaid work requirements in Nebraska. People are worried
item.description: Starting May 1, many people covered by Medicaid in Nebraska have to prove they are working. It's a requirement most states will have to implement under President Trump's budget law, beginning in January.
item.content:encoded:
Starting May 1, many people covered by Medicaid in Nebraska have to prove they are working. It's a requirement most states will have to implement under President Trump's budget law, beginning in January.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: Phil Galewitz

item.title: American Public Health Association CEO on Trump's latest nominee for surgeon general
item.description: The president's new pick for surgeon general has been both supportive and critical of MAHA health policy. NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Dr. Georges Benjamin, CEO of the American Public Health Association.
item.content:encoded: The president's new pick for surgeon general has been both supportive and critical of MAHA health policy. NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Dr. Georges Benjamin, CEO of the American Public Health Association.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Trump pulls Casey Means' stalled surgeon general nomination, announces new pick
item.description: President Trump says he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump says he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: In real-world test, an AI model did better than doctors at diagnosing patients
item.description: Researchers evaluated how well an AI model could diagnose and make decisions about patient care in the ER.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers evaluated how well an AI model could diagnose and make decisions about patient care in the ER.

(Image credit: shapecharge/E+)
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: To catch colorectal cancer early, advocates push to make 'poop talk' OK
item.description: It's a very treatable form of cancer if caught early, yet younger adults rarely get screened. Patient advocates want more people to talk to their doctors about risk factors and number two.
item.content:encoded: It's a very treatable form of cancer if caught early, yet younger adults rarely get screened. Patient advocates want more people to talk to their doctors about risk factors and number two.
item.dc:creator: Yuki Noguchi

item.title: RFK pushes abstinence and wellness programs he says helped him recover from addiction
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy is promoting abstinence and 12-step addiction treatment programs that he says helped him recover from heroin use.
item.content:encoded: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy is promoting abstinence and 12-step addiction treatment programs that he says helped him recover from heroin use.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: A look at one of the farms RFK Jr. has pointed to as a model for 'reparenting'
item.description: The U.S. health secretary says he wants to shift addiction care toward an approach that includes rural farms or camps for people in recovery. Many addiction experts say the idea is outdated.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. health secretary says he wants to shift addiction care toward an approach that includes rural farms or camps for people in recovery. Many addiction experts say the idea is outdated.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann
